summary: A solid 1973 show highlighted by a sparkling China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition and an inspired Here Comes Sunshine jam; recording quality isn’t described but appears listenable.
review: Reviewers rave about the tight chord work from Phil, Keith and Bob during the rare “Mind Left Body Jam” that bridges China Cat Sunflower into I Know You Rider, and they praise the extended space‑noodle on Here Comes Sunshine as well as the seamless Weather Report Suite → Eyes of the World segue.  The performance is described as a bit fast but overall enjoyable, while no specific recording details are given.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently positive comments about musical highlights and tight playing outweigh the lack of recording‑quality information, resulting in a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No reviewers mentioned soundboard vs. audience or clarity; assume an acceptable but unremarkable source.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Set is noted as a bit fast but flows smoothly, with memorable segues such as Weather Report Suite into Eyes of the World and a beautiful closing with We Bid You Goodnight.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia receives a “beautiful harmonies and a Garcia send off” mention in the closing.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for splendid chord transitions during the Mind Left Body Jam.
band_member_comments.Bob: Also highlighted for his role in the impressive chord work of the jam.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s contribution noted in the chord transition; “space noodle” on Here Comes Sunshine praised as a keyboard highlight.
